Class VelocityVector

java.lang.Object
car.VelocityVector
public class VelocityVector extends Object
VelocityVector.java Code Description: Represents a 2D vector used for movement and physics calculations. Stores x and y components and provides basic vector operations such as addition, scaling, magnitude, and limiting speed.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    VelocityVector()
    Creates a vector with zero magnitude.
    VelocityVector(double x, double y)
    Creates a vector with specified x and y components.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    add(double x, double y)
    Adds values to the vector components.
    double
    getAngle()
    Returns the direction (angle) of the vector in radians.
    double
    getMagnitude()
    Calculates the magnitude (length) of the vector.
    double
    getXComp()
    Returns the x-component of the vector.
    double
    getYComp()
    Returns the y-component of the vector.
    void
    limit(double max)
    Limits the magnitude of the vector to a maximum value.
    void
    rotateTo(double angle)
    Rotates the vector to a specified angle while preserving magnitude.
    void
    scale(double factor)
    Scales the vector by a constant factor.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • VelocityVector

      public VelocityVector()
      Creates a vector with zero magnitude.

    • VelocityVector

      public VelocityVector(double x, double y)
      Creates a vector with specified x and y components.
      Parameters:
      x - x-component of the vector
      y - y-component of the vector

  • Method Details

    • getXComp

      public double getXComp()
      Returns the x-component of the vector.
      Returns:
      x-component value

    • getYComp

      public double getYComp()
      Returns the y-component of the vector.
      Returns:
      y-component value

    • add

      public void add(double x, double y)
      Adds values to the vector components. Often used to apply acceleration or force.
      Parameters:
      x - amount added to the x-component
      y - amount added to the y-component

    • scale

      public void scale(double factor)
      Scales the vector by a constant factor. Commonly used to simulate friction or drag.
      Parameters:
      factor - value to multiply both components by

    • getMagnitude

      public double getMagnitude()
      Calculates the magnitude (length) of the vector.
      Returns:
      magnitude of the vector

    • getAngle

      public double getAngle()
      Returns the direction (angle) of the vector in radians.
      Returns:
      angle of the vector in radians

    • limit

      public void limit(double max)
      Limits the magnitude of the vector to a maximum value. If the vector exceeds the maximum speed, it is scaled down while preserving direction.
      Parameters:
      max - maximum allowed magnitude

    • rotateTo

      public void rotateTo(double angle)
      Rotates the vector to a specified angle while preserving magnitude. This is useful for aligning velocity with a new direction.
      Parameters:
      angle - new direction in radians
